name: Run, Run, Run (As Fast As You Can!)

text.content:
Run, Run, Run (As Fast As You Can!)

Friend Opportunity: Cousin Radu

Radu invites the PCs to his bowling league, and can use the time to share his Streetwise knowledge with them.

Enemy Opportunity: Maelstrom

Of all the crazies out there, the UK madlass has hired a local branch of Maelstrom for muscle.

Rep Opportunity: Aim To Head

If you take an aimed shot at Emily Dunnes from a stealthy position and manage to hit, she drops dead immediately.

Gig Payout

0.01 % finder's fee of the hardware crypto wallet. This is equal to 600eb + 3 Eagles each.

Notes to the referee

This gig does not feature much pre-written dialogue. At this point, all the important NPCs from the Afterlife should have already made an appearance. For this reason, only information that the NPCs possess is given.

The basic direction of the adventure is set, but feel free to adjust it based on the decisions and actions of the PCs, your specific needs while running it and / or to add more content to different scenes. As a longtime referee for Cyberpunk 2020 and Cyberpunk Red, I prefer a high level of flexibility and the mission should be handled accordingly. The minimalistic approach gives you the opportunity to learn more about important skills like improvisation and sandboxing. If the PCs are very experienced / less experienced in combat, you can always adjust the recommended number of enemies within the different beats.

Please keep in mind that all mentioned skill checks are merely suggestions. In case your players come up with a better or different idea, please go for it and adjust.

Example: The players ask you if they might be able to open a locked door without the proper tools at hand. A good answer to that is always: "Yes, but…"

Credits

This gig is dedicated to Sir Brian Walsh. You are the best friend one could ask for.
- Jess Matthews

Written by Jess Matthews. Edited by Prometheus.

Thanks to: Anthony [Nightlight editorial] and Prometheus [feedback & map making tutorials]

Special thanks to: Cataphiles [music] ( https://cataphiles.bandcamp.com/album/cataphiles-4 )

Solo project of the bass player: ( https://atticfrost.bandcamp.com/ )

Please share the links with your players if they like the music from the Hideout scene to support the the band(s)!

Beat credits

  • Beat 1: Hobnail Boots - Meet me on the corner

  • Beat 2: Hocico - Where angels don't sing

  • Sub Beat 2.3: Nirvana - Where did you sleep last night

  • Beat 3: The White Stripes - There's no home for you here

  • Beat 4: Project Pitchfork - Crash

  • Beat 5: Cataphiles - Where to hide

  • Sub Beat 5.3: Nine Inch Nails - We're in this together now

  • Beat 6: Merciful Nuns - Saturn calls

  • Beat 7: Killing Joke - Age of greed

  • Sub Beat 7.3: Skold - Devil inside

  • Beat 8: Unkle - Rabbit in your headlights

Beat chart

Beat 1: Meet me on the corner

Instead of Sprinter, the group is greeted by Sammy the next time they go to the Afterlife, who explains how Sprinter rushed in earlier to grab some of her belongings and left in a hurry. Rogue and the other staff want to know what's going on; Sprinter is supposed to work tonight.

Beat 2: Where angels don't sing

The PCs are sent out to find their missing merc via Rogue, who provides the crew with Sprinter's address and license plate number.

Beat 3: There's no home for you here

The crew investigates Sprinter's home, discovers a break-in and finds a clue where she might be.

Beat 4: Crash

According to the screamsheets, Sprinter's car crashed and burned on Interstate 5 - investigating it provides clues, though the car's driver is nowhere to be found.

Beat 5: Where to hide

One clue lets the crew investigate a dive bar called "The Hideout". After defeating a gang-related attack, they are allowed to browse through Sprinter's old belongings up in the attic.

"The Hideout" bears fruit in the form of a mandate for a safe deposit box as well as some old clippings of a drug deal gone wrong in the UK.

Beat 6: Saturn calls

Say hello again to the bank you recently robbed! The safe deposit box contains a black suitcase with a cryptocurrency wallet inside, and shortly after the crew finds it, Sprinter finally makes contact with them to arrange a meeting for which she never arrives.

Beat 7: Age of greed

The crew discovers that their car/gear/clothing/HQ has been bugged. They need to lure the culprit with the drug money or come up with another ruse.

Beat 8: Head like a hole

Following the antagonist, the crew drives to an old shack in the middle of the Badlands. There they find Sprinter held hostage and can deal with the culprits once and for all.

The FM receiver crackles, the wavelength carrying a cool, seductive female voice…

Nightlight: “Thank you for subscribing to the Night City Audio Screamsheet Program. This is Nightlight, the voice of the city, here to take the scream off the sheet and into your ears for all you night owls without enough time to read. The date is XX/XX/2045, and the weather is going to be a smooth, Central Californian 91 degrees. Let's begin our news day…

My dear night owls, how are you all holding up? Well, I hope you're ready to get out, with our sports season coming up. The Night City Slammers, Heat, and DeathDealers are all roaring to go crush some LA losers - tickets now available at your local fixer. Get them ordered and give our boys and girls in the Night City Red your support. Onto the news with Batty Carlson. Batty?”

Batty: “Well hello there Nightlight! Night City, are you aware of our current foreign gang problem? You aren't? Well FUCK YOUUUU! These foreign euro-gangs are infiltrating your neighborhood protection racket to get into YOUR pockets, and with the high level of immigrants from the UK-”

Nightlight: “Quite the intriguing thought Batty, but might I kindly remind you that we are talking about current news lines?”

Batty: “Uh… ahem. Yes, Nightlight. The big news for today: four ships from the UK have just docked in Port 9 of the Upper Marina. This is the largest recorded fleet of ships that have arrived since the NC holocaust, not to mention the first ones to make it past the mines this month, and it has night citizens nervous.”

Nervous Night Citizen: “I’m nervous man, I’m real fucking nervous! One moment it was no ships, the next moment it was ship tiiiimmme! I’ve never seen a fucking ship in my life! Get that fuzzy black dildo out of my face, I don’t swing that way, baby!” [Obvious sounds of someone jumping off a boardwalk into the ocean]

Nightlight: “Well… wow Batty you didn’t cut. Any of that.” (sigh) “More on this story, after the break.”

pages.name: Beat 1: Meet Me On The Corner

text.content:
This mission makes use of the @UUID[Scene.s4clZm7hY4mfQllb]{Afterlife Lobby,} @UUID[Scene.nFr548DTPrIrheUt]{Sprinter's Gaff,} @UUID[Scene.HnzME5DTIG7nVlAW]{The Hideout,} @UUID[Scene.vboI8hWM5vr7ZfIB]{Bank of Night City,} and @UUID[Scene.bmnqkZgq3dKn5eJY]{Badlands Captive} maps, along with several environments. To begin:

  • Place tokens for @UUID[Actor.rQdrdTOYV13yilZD]{Sammy,} @UUID[Actor.5jUfSTLGzd7EEBWX]{The Owner,} and other Afterlife NPCs (GM discretion) to the Afterlife Lobby scene. Sammy is standing behind the concierge's desk, while Rogue is off to the side.

  • Place half a dozen or so civilians in a crowd around the Concierge's desk.

  • Place the player tokens outside the Afterlife's front doors.

  • Change the scene to the Afterlife Lobby.

Then, if/once players have made their payments for entry, read the following:

Trouble In Heaven

Once the Afterlife's doors slide open, the PCs feel a cool, dry, air-conditioned relief from the city's heat and humidity. Inside, though, an unusual scene greets them.

Gone is their usual chipper concierge, for the first time since they've started working for the club. In Sprinter's place behind the concierge's desk is a haggard looking Sammy, and a not-too-small crowd of disconcerted guests who are swarming him like Piranhas around a keg of Smash.

The PCs overhear the guests expressing their dissatisfaction with the most recent service while Sammy is obviously not the best temp to help out in a customer service role. Most reasonable people would think he was right to snap back at them in disbelief, but the Ice Queen of the Afterlife, Rogue, thinks otherwise. With her cold and distant voice, she reminds him about the Afterlife's goal to achieve unsurpassed customer satisfaction and to take the guests' concerns more seriously.

It's at this point that Sammy spots the party. "Hey!" he shouts, and separates himself from his cloud of problems. The guests look on uncertainly as he all but runs across the lobby towards them.

He asks them if they are busy that night since he's got a fire going and needs them to put it out in exchange for preem pay due to short notice.

Explaining The Situation

  • Sammy's quick to explain that Sprinter has gone missing.

    • The circumstances of her flight are unknown - all anyone knows is thanks to the security cameras, which showed her running into the lobby, and a few minutes later, sprinting back out of it with a hastily-packed holdall.

  • Sammy's worried about the behavior.

    • In all the time he has known her, Sprinter has been nothing but dedicated to the organisation they mutually work for, and has never before failed to report on-shift.

    • And while impulsive, Sprinter's always been noted as anything but thoughtless - whatever unexpected event triggered the sudden flight, it must have been huge.

  • Both Sammy and Rogue are sure it's serious.

    • The security footage can be provided; a DV17 Human Perception check reveals that Sprinter is in a panic and at least a little frightened.

  • Sammy thinks it might have something to do with her past.

    • Sprinter's not ever said much about where she came from, but Sammy knows she has some kind of family back home.

Once the conversation is concluded, Sammy is all but dragged away by irate customers of the Afterlife, and he directs the team towards Rogue for more information.

Still at the Afterlife

When the PCs head in Rogue's direction, she notices them immediately. In the meantime, she has already taken a seat in her lounge and beckons them over. The surrounding staff immediately clear the way.

Seats and drinks are provided. Rogue remarks that Sammy has probably already informed them of the situation. She would like to have her concierge back before the place goes completely down the drain.

Unfortunately, the Afterlife is currently in some disarray, so none of the regular staff can investigate Sprinter's disappearance.

In addition to the footage from the security cameras, Rogue places a microchip in the centre of the table with additional information about the Afterlife's concierge. The situation is serious and this is reflected in the already cool expression on the Queen of the Afterlife's face.

When the PCs bring up the subject of payment, Rogue immediately brushes it off. First things first. This matter is urgend and any minute wasted is one too many.

After receiving the information, Rogue urges swift action from the PCs and makes herself apologise. Important business. It's a familiar story.

Gathering intel from the chip

  • The chip can be slotted into any agent or chipware socket. It contains two valuable pieces of information regarding Sprinter.

    • Her gaff is located at 221 Brugha Lane in Little Europe, Apartment 2B.

    • She drives a bright orange Quadra Turbo-R with the licence plate NC-21-11.

Where did you sleep last night?

If the PCs want to search for Sprinter's car first, skip to Beat 4: Crash and then to Beat 3: There's no home for you here. Otherwise, stick to the original order.

pages.name: Beat 3: There's no home for you here

text.content:
First, prepare the scene of Sprinter's Gaff.

  • Place Emily Dunnes' token at the top center of the apartment building on the grey fire escape.

  • The door to Sprinter's apartment at the bottom left should already be open. The door at the top center of the fire escape is also open.

  • Optionally, a second car be placed on the curb in front of the alley to the left of the apartment block.

  • Add a good number of civillians to the streets in front of the building.

  • The player character tokens can be placed anywhere on the streets.

  • Activate the Little Europe Environment now and read the following:

Sprinter's gaff

Little Europe is definitely one of the more upmarket neighbourhoods and, compared to the rest of Night City, its residents can be considered the well-off upper middle class. You, being Edgerunners, armed and wearing armour, do stand out here.

Between the hustle and bustle of passers-by, you can tell that this neighbourhood has seen better times, but definitely worse. It's a place where you can take a deep breath and unwind. The address, 221 Brugha Lane, is a medium-sized flat block in fairly decent condition.

Now activate the scene Sprinter's Gaff.

The PCs have to take the elevator to the second floor of the building. When the doors open, they see a hooded figure on the fire escape right in front of them. It hastily climbs and jumps down, rushes through the alley and drives off in a car. If the PCs want to give chase, they must pass a professional check on Athletics, otherwise they injure themselves when jumping off the fire ladder (2D6 - armour is ignored). With a bit of luck and professional checks on Perception, they can still recognise the license plate. Note that any bonus from suitable cyber optics or gear may be relevant here.

If the PCs later find out who the car is registered to, they will discover that it is a rental car from a company near the airport of Sacramento.

It is easy to see that the door to Sprinter's apartment has been forced open and the gaff itself hastily searched. It is difficult to tell whether anything of value or interest has been removed.

If the PCs are not reasonably discreet in their search, the neighbours, now back from work, will call the police, who should arrive in 1D10 +3 rounds.

The furnishings in the gaff must have once been very exclusive and beautiful, but unfortunately Adam Smasher himself has obviously vented his frustration over a lost bet here. Nothing is left where it should be.

A professional check on Perception or a difficult check on Reveal Object lets the PCs find a damaged digital photo frame next to the bedside table. It has a crack in the display, switching to other pictures no longer works, but it shows the last picture viewed:

Sprinter is standing in front of a dive bar with another person. According to the neon-coloured and flickering sign, it is called "The Hideout". Next to Sprinter, you can see a male person a few years younger who bears a striking resemblance to her.

This is a good time to show your players the character artwork of the NPC Franklin Cross.

To find out the address of the "The Hideout", a professional Streetwise check is required. Alternatively, they could ask a combat cab driver / Fixer for a fee (19 Dame Street). The dive bar is located at the very edge of Little Europe near the border to the Hot Zone.

Activate the the Central New Westbrook Environment.

Gathering information

A Media, Fixer or Lawman may want to ask their contacts about Sprinter's license plate. Alternatively, a professional Library Search check would also be possible in relation to the brand new screamsheets. Maybe the PCs want to investigate the crash site themselves? This is what apparently happened:

There was a serious traffic accident on Interstate 5 towards Sacramento. It is possibly connected to an armed attack on the vehicle. The car was heavily fueled with lead and burned out upside down. However, there is no trace of the driver. Blood samples were recovered and are now being analysed by the NCPD's laboratory.

Use the Hideout map.

  • Place Cousin Radu's token behind the bar and fill the place with any number of random guests (Civillians / Street Thugs / Gangsters).

  • Place two 6th Street Privates on the street in front of the venue.

  • Replace the 6th Streets' weapons with shotguns using the Martial skill and add shotgun slugs for them.

  • Place the NPCs Jim, Lou, Mike & Pete at the top right corner of the Hideout bar within the animated lights (they are playing a concert there).

  • The player character tokens can be placed on the street at the very top of the scene.

  • During the course of the evening, 1-2 vehicles with mixed level Maelstrom Gangers will appear in front of the building.

  • The recommended number of enemies is PCs +1.

  • Read the following:

The Hideout

"The Hideout" is a dive bar as you would expect. Neon advertisements for cheap drinks flicker outside. On the street, you are immediately greeted by members of the 6th Street Gang with shotguns at the ready. They make it unmistakably clear to you with just a look that no conflict is welcome here. Their bodies protected by Kevlar.

Apparently a concert is taking place indoors. A deep post punk bass blends into the screaming crescendos of an electric guitar. The drummer supports the keyboard playing singer at the front with rock interludes that set the beat.

Indoors the PCs are greeted by the local drink slinger named Cousin Radu. He is a slightly chubby barkeep with a well groomed beard and side parting dark hair. He's wearing what can only be described as the flashiest, and probably ugliest, colourful shirt the PCs may have ever encountered. His personality is loud and outgoing with a chatty and friendly nature.

This is a good opportunity to show Cousin Radu's character artwork to the players!

Before the PCs can even ask their own questions or order drinks, he immediately engages them in small talk of the worst kind. Where they are from - after all, this is the first time he has seen them here. Whether they watched the Night City Slammers' tragedy against the Los Angeles Hellbats last night. What their favourite fashion brand is. Every now and then he also drops a short, crude joke at the expense of a PC during his stories. However, he is also very good at dealing with counterattacks and can laugh about himself. Someone with a knack for lingos might realise that his accent is from somewhere in Eastern Europe. Feel free to make it difficult for the PCs to get to the point. There's so much Cousin Radu would love to talk about!

When the PCs can finally ask him questions about Sprinter, his eyes light up. Of course he knows Sprinter! Are they friends of Sprinter? The next round is on the house! Is she coming over tonight as well? Oh, they're looking for her? No, unfortunately, she hasn't been here lately. She works way too much and barely got the time to pop by for a lovely pint of her favourite strawberry cider...

This is the moment when the car(s) halt outside with screeching tires. Shots are immediately fired between the members of Rainbow Road on guard duty and Maelstrom's nightmare figures.

As soon as they notice something odd, the band members grab their gear and run out the back of the Hideout, escaping to the west.

You should now remove the music from the scene. Menu -> Ambient Sound Controls -> click on the Ambient Sound Object and then DEL to do so. Also stop the background sounds in the playlist tab of Foundry.

The PCs can now rush outside and attack Maelstrom, protecting the patrons and staff at the Hideout.

After the fight, Cousin Radu is very grateful for the PCs' intervention. He remembers that Sprinter stored some personal belongings in the Hideout's attic a long while ago. In case it helps them finding her, he allows them to look at her precious possessions.

If the crew has fled from Maelstrom, more members of the Rainbow Road will appear and take down the attackers after a prolonged exchange of gunshots and slashes. However, Cousin Radu will no longer offer the group any help, won't befriend anyone even if Sprinter is rescued eventually, and they will probably have to break into "The Hideout" after hours to acquire more information.

We're in this together now

The PCs find the following items in a rusty metal box in the dusty attic:

  • A mandate for a safe deposit box issued by Tara Cross. The safe deposit box is located at the Bank of Night City branch in Little Europe.

  • Do the PCs know the real name of Sprinter by now? If not, try to be vague about it while the PCs try to put the different pieces of information together.

  • Several printed screamsheet articles with photos from British media. There are reports from Birmingham about a large-scale drug deal that went horribly wrong. One gang member and two police officers were fatally wounded in the raid. The suitcase with the alleged cryptocurrency hardware wallet was actually just full of old printed gossip rags from the early 2030s. Both gangs accused each other of having exchanged and hidden the device beforehand. After all, they would all have to go to prison for a long time. Only the young lover of the gangster in charge, Emily Dunnes - who was also present at the scene, was classified as unsuspicious after a short time and was able to leave the police station after 48 hours of questioning. On closer inspection (professional Perception check), this young beau looks highly familiar to the PCs: It's the person from the photo with Sprinter posing outside "The Hideout" - his name is listed as Franklin Cross.

Use the Night City Bank scene from Mission 3 - Robbin The Hood.

  • Place the player tokens on the street outside the bank.

  • Place 6 RatSec Grunts at the entrance, keeping watch on the crowds.

  • Place 3 RatSec Elite and 1/2 partycount RatSec Grunts in the southernmost portion of the admin area of the bank (where the couch is), keeping an eye on the security cameras.

  • Place 3/4 partycount RatSec Execs around the scene at your discretion.

  • For every other actor in the scene, place up to 3 civilians, representing bank staff and customers.

Bank of Night City

It is already late in the evening and so the PCs cannot visit the Bank of Night City in Little Europe until the next morning. The spare time can be used to relax or further research.

The Bank of Night City branch is now heavily guarded. It might have something to do with a recent robbery (see: Mission 3 - Robbin The Hood). There are very thorough checks for weapons. These must be left within a locker in a transition area on the way to the main room. They are scanned for potentially dangerous cyberware and PCs who have it must wear a bracelet for the duration of their visit, which disables the cyberware in question.

Due to the recent heist, before entering, let the PCs roll on their Personal Grooming skills in order to see how good their disguises are. If they fail, they might save the day by some decent Acting checks. This leads to two different outcomes. If they fail, continue with Option 1. If, however, they are successful in their deception, skip to Option 2.

Option 1.) The PCs are screwed! An attentive bank employee or security guard recognises their amateurish attempt at deception. The bank's alarm is activated and, of course, the NCPD also receives a message about what appears to be the second robbery attempt within a very short time by the same perpetrators. How can anyone be so stupid and greedy? The bank employees slightly shake their heads in disbelief. Feel free to make the ensuing combat encounter as tough as nails.

The PCs should now disappear as quickly as possible (Bombshell? Bombshell!) and have to obtain the contents of the safe deposit box via a trustworthy person or hired middleman.

To do this, they must of course spend money, contact a fixer and hand over the mandate. Hopefully this goes well and the middleman doesn't disappear with the desired goods.

Option 2.) Depending on the appearance of the PCs, the staff will treat them with anything from professional neutrality to arrogant disdain. It may take some Persuasion checks to convince the employees of the legitimacy of their mandate, but in the end they are led to the basement. There they are handed the suitcase from the safe deposit box and then kindly asked to leave.

Devil inside

The PCs can now find a quiet place to inspect the contents of the suitcase. Inside is the cryptocurrency hardware wallet mentioned in the screamsheat articles. Without the correct code, it is almost impossible to gain access to the device's contents, though.

A short time later, Sprinter reports to one of the PCs from a disposable cell phone. Contrary to her otherwise professional demeanor, she appears extremely stressed, hectic and exhausted. She wants to meet with the PCs at the arcade on Moore Street as soon as possible. She gives no further information on the phone and hangs up. A professional check on Human Perception suggests that she is very worried about persecutors.

The meeting is supposed to take place 90 minutes after the call. However, Sprinter does not show up. The PCs can wait at the meeting point for as long as they like.

Use the Dystopian City Junction or Dystopian City Streets scene from the Cyberpunk RED Core Maps.

  • Place the PC tokens, their vehicle token (if available) anywhere you like.

  • Add a about 10-12 Civiallians / Street Thugs to the scene.

  • If the disguised Maelstrom member's cover is blown, pick a random Maelstrom Gang member Token from the mook pack to replace it.

  • Activate the scene.

  • In case a fight with four more Maelstrom members ensues, use a mixed level variety from the mook pack later on.

Near Moore Street arcade

After the failed meeting with Sprinter, the PCs probably want to discuss things elsewhere. If they have their own vehicle at their disposal, they will see that someone has tampered with their car a few seconds ago when leaving the arcade. At this point, ask for some very tough Perception or difficult Reveal Object checks.

If the crew does not have a vehicle at their disposal, one PC is bumped into by some random stranger on the street who attaches a bug to their clothing. The person's intentions can be seen through with an appropriate skill.

The unknown individual is a member of Maelstrom who has disguised themselves as a homeless person.

If the crew is still carrying the suitcase around, four mixed level members of Maelstrom will try to attack the PCs and steal it from them instead. In each case, a ganger also tries to attach a bug to a PC's clothing during melee combat.

Sooner or later, a difficult check on Reveal Object should tell the PCs that their vehicle or clothing has been bugged. If this does not happen, sooner or later the crew will realise that they are being constantly followed by a dark car with tinted windows.

From this point on, the crew can think of a ruse to lure the opponents and become the pursuers themselves. There should be no limits to creativity here.

Two of many possible ways to figure out the whereabouts of the opponents' HQ:

  • Having a fake conversation while the bugs are active on where they want to stash the the suitcase. Then they can wait in hiding for Maelstrom to show up there and tail them back to the Badlands. Let's hope the crew is smart enough to remove the device from the suitcase first…

  • Interrogate one of the surviving Maelstrom members from the ambush.

Use the Badlands Captive map. Place the tokens of Emily Dunnes and an equal to the groups' size number of toughened Maelstrom members within the building.

  • Sprinter's token is placed in the locked room at the top right.

  • Reduce her HP to 15.

  • The player character tokens should be placed at the bottom of the scene.

Badlands Captive

The crew now has to try to free Sprinter from the hands of the enemy. The trail leads them to a secret base in the Badlands.

Burning piles of trash and a campfire illuminate the way to an old chop shop in the middle of the Californian desert. The building has an entrance to the left, a garage door at the bottom right and several windows that can be pried open.

When the PCs peek through the windows, difficult or professional Stealth checks are made by the crew.

This is a good moment to remind your players of the reputation opportunity mentioned in the mission summary. You can now show the character artwork of Emily Dunnes to them.

Once all enemies have been incapacitated or killed and Sprinter has been rescued, she gratefully reports on the background of what happened:

  • Her brother Franklin was in a temporary fling with Emily Dunnes in Birmingham, UK.

  • Due to the toxic relationship, he planned to sabotage a drug deal of his girlfriend's and abscond with the money.

  • He asked Sprinter to stash the suitcase for him until the dust had settled.

  • However, Sprinter thought it was wrong to leave the money to Franklin and took the suitcase to Night City, where she hid it in the safe deposit box.

  • Franklin was less than enthusiastic about his sister's actions and temporarily broke off contact. Eventually, however, the two reconciled.

  • After Emily Dunnes escaped from prison, she threatened Franklin, but did not dare to murder him in cold blood. He told her that Sprinter had hidden the money.

  • Emily Dunnes therefore made her way to Night City and hired Maelstrom as backup.

  • Sprinter found out too late from screamsheets back home that Emily Dunnes had managed to flee and wanted to make her way to the airport in Sacramento to fly back to Birmingham in order to help her brother.

  • On the way to the airport, she was run off the road by two cars and then engaged in combat by Maelstrom. She managed to escape fairly injured. Shortly before the meeting with the crew at the arcade, she was unfortunately overpowered in an ambush and taken hostage.

Sprinter will of course invite the PCs to a fitting victory celebration at the Afterlife. The contents of the suitcase are handed over to the British authorities and the crew receive a finder's fee and three eagles each.
